name: Wes Montgomery
abstract: John Leslie "Wes" Montgomery (March 6, 1923 – June 15, 1968) was an American jazz guitarist. He is most known for his unusual technique of plucking the strings with the side of his thumb and for his extensive use of octaves, which gave him a distinctive sound.

about_quotes.speaker: Nick Brignola
about_quotes.evidence: So I went in and I was in the men’s room and Wes Montgomery came in and I knew who he was, so I said to him, “I know your brothers and I just got through playing with them.” And he said, “Well get your horn and come on up.” So I wound up playing with Wes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nick Brignola interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 28, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Magnarelli
about_quotes.evidence: I used to play with a great drummer, Jimmy Loveless. You know he’s a New York guy, he played with Wes Montgomery and was on some of his records.

about_quotes.speaker: Vincent Pelote
about_quotes.evidence: he became my hero... I should always think about that more... Of course I’d have to have a Wes Montgomery record
about_quotes.doc_title: Vincent Pelote interviewed by Monk Rowe, Orlando, Florida, February 25, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Henry Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: the bass player brought a record of Wes Montgomery — “Tequila” — and from then on I was listening and I said well what is this... So I got that record, and then everybody that was on that record I went and got their records... And after that I started really listening to a lot of jazz, and wanted to
about_quotes.doc_title: Henry Johnson interviewed by Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 30, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Sheryl Bailey
about_quotes.evidence: I heard Wes Montgomery. And then I just was hooked. It was an addiction from there... I came across this box of transcription I had done... there were stacks, and there was a ton of Clifford Brown, Dexter Gordon, and Wes
about_quotes.doc_title: Sheryl Bailey interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 24, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: John Abercrombie
about_quotes.evidence: And he came to Berklee, we all met him, and the instructor at the time, Jack Peterson, had this little guitar ensemble... So we were going to play this arrangement and let Wes solo on it.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001
